citations.desc: Attending veterinarian and adequate veterinary care (dealers and exhibitors).
citations.narrative:
The macaw observed in the round pen during this inspection has extensive feather loss. The feather loss is primarily on
the neck and chest. The facility indicates that when the birds are moved inside for the winter the feather plucking worsens
but there is no effective plan in place to address or minimize this recurring condition. Causes of feather loss may include
distress, incompatibility and presence of ectoparasites and other reasons.
The bird's environment, housing and health shall be re-evaluated to determine cause and a plan put in place to provide
relief and protect the health and well-being of the macaw.
Correct by April 20, 2026

***A child was bitten by a sloth during a public interaction event. With the attendant present the facility allows interactions
with the sloth by having the customer sit and placing the sloth in their lap. The customers are instructed to keep their
faces away from the sloth. The manager stated that during the interaction the attendant was talking to the parent
answering a question when the child attempted to lean in to kiss or hug the sloth when the sloth bit the child on the face.
The child received treatment for a small wound on her chin.
The facility failed to provide sufficient distance and or barrier between the public and the sloth to protect the public from
harm. The manager stated the facility has discontinued public interactions with the sloth and the sloth has been placed in
quarantine.
During public exhibition, any animal must be handled so there is minimal risk of harm to the animal and to the public, with
sufficient distance and/or barriers between the animal and the general viewing public so as to assure the safety of animals
and the public.
Correct by April 20, 2026.

***The hoofed stock shelter on the top side of the drive through is in disrepair. The shelter was damaged by high winds
and flipped over. The structure was stood back up but it is leaning and has torn aluminum panels peeling off of the top
and side. The the legs do not appear to be completely secured to the ground leaving it leaning and subject to collapse
with the wind. There is also a wooden structure in the enclosure that has pieces of aluminum/sheets that are rolled and
are peeling off of the roof and broken boards on the ground.
As the structures collapse or pieces of metal with sharp edges fall onto the ground the hoofed stock may come in contact
with it and become injured. The structures shall be repaired or replaced to protect the health and welfare of the animals.
This inspection and exit interview were conducted with the Manager. Exit briefing conducted with the president by phone,
n

narrative:
2.40(b)(3) 
   
Attending veterinarian and adequate veterinary care (dealers and exhibitors). 
The macaw observed in the round pen during this inspection has extensive feather loss. The feather loss is primarily on 
the neck and chest. The facility indicates that when the birds are moved inside for the winter the feather plucking worsens 
but there is no effective plan in place to address or minimize this recurring condition. Causes of feather loss may include 
distress, incompatibility and presence of ectoparasites and other reasons.  
 
The bird's environment, housing and health shall be re-evaluated to determine cause and a plan put in place to provide 
relief and protect the health and well-being of the macaw. 
 
Correct by April 20, 2026 
2.131(c)(1)  Critical
   
Handling of animals. 
***A child was bitten by a sloth during a public interaction event. With the attendant present the facility allows interactions 
with the sloth by having the customer sit and placing the sloth in their lap. The customers are instructed to keep their 
faces away from the sloth. The manager stated that during the interaction the attendant was talking to the parent 
answering a question when the child attempted to lean in to kiss or hug the sloth when the sloth bit the child on the face. 
The child received treatment for a small wound on her chin.  
 
The facility failed to provide sufficient distance and or barrier between the public and the sloth to protect the public from 
harm. The manager stated the facility has discontinued public interactions with the sloth and the sloth has been placed in 
quarantine.  
 
During public exhibition, any animal must be handled so there is minimal risk of harm to the animal and to the public, with 
sufficient distance and/or barriers between the animal and the general viewing public so as to assure the safety of animals 
and the public. 
 
Correct by April 20, 2026.

3.125(a)  Repeat
   
Facilities, general. 
***The hoofed stock shelter on the top side of the drive through is in disrepair. The shelter was damaged by high winds 
and flipped over. The structure was stood back up but it is leaning and has torn aluminum panels peeling off of the top 
and side. The the legs do not appear to be completely secured to the ground leaving it leaning and subject to collapse 
with the wind. There is also a wooden structure in the enclosure that has pieces of aluminum/sheets that are rolled and 
are peeling off of the roof and broken boards on the ground. 
 
As the structures collapse or pieces of metal with sharp edges fall onto the ground the hoofed stock may come in contact 
with it and become injured. The structures shall be repaired or replaced to protect the health and welfare of the animals. 
 
This inspection and exit interview were conducted with the Manager. Exit briefing conducted with the president by phone,
